response.justification: Experienced senior engineer with strong systems thinking, low-latency service work (DLang at Sociomantic), and genuine geospatial domain experience via GISCollective. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ work a decade ago at National Instruments and no Rust at all. Recent work is TypeScript/Node-heavy. Worth a screening call only if the team values geospatial domain knowledge and is open to a candidate ramping up on C++/Rust.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience through long-running GISCollective platform
Low-latency, high-throughput service experience with async I/O and fibers at Sociomantic
Senior breadth across web, embedded, and Linux server applications with strong TDD/CI discipline
concerns: No demonstrated modern C++ experience; last C/C++ role was ~2015 and pre-modern
No Rust experience listed anywhere in the resume or projects
No navigation, routing, or map tiling pipeline experience
